metadata.title: Skill File Anatomy: Build Once, Invoke Every Semester

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. I keep re-typing my grading workflow every two weeks, so I want to freeze it into a SKILL.md instead of digging through old chat windows. Walk me through building one for lesson-plan generation that asks for objectives and grade level first. Then: (1) draft the frontmatter with a trigger and decide auto-invoke versus explicit slash command; (2) write the workflow block, Never section, and Verification block; (3) point out which of my Never rules, like never generate a final grade, actually belong in a hook instead.

Walk me through building one for lesson-plan generation that asks for objectives and grade level first. Then: (1) draft the frontmatter with a trigger and decide auto-invoke versus explicit slash command; (2) write the workflow block, Never section, and Verification block; (3) point out which of my Never rules, like never generate a final grade, actually belong in a hook instead.
props.segment: Skill File Anatomy: Build Once, Invoke

beats.narration_text: This is Musinique. Teachers re-specify the same grading workflow every two weeks. Two hundred and eighty words, typed from memory, slightly different each time, because the last version is buried in a chat window they cannot find. A SKILL.md file stores the workflow once. Slash grading-workflow runs it in ninety seconds instead of eighteen minutes. That is the whole idea.

Claude reads the SKILL.md file, asks for the learning objectives and grade level, and produces a lesson plan saved to lesson-plans slash. The context cost of the skill is zero until you invoke it — it is not loaded at session start. First invocation: forty-five minutes to refine. Every invocation after: ninety seconds.
beats.new_visual_element: SKILL.md loads on invocation — 90 sec vs 18 min timer comparison

beats.narration_text: You don't need a 400-line system prompt to manage your release calendar. You need a 15-line core that knows what the job is, and skill modules it loads when the job gets specific — the release checklist, the pitch template, the playlist-scam detector. Decompose once, and the machine gets cheaper every run. That's the same arithmetic whether you're running inventory for a warehouse or release cycles for a back catalog.

artifactLines: The same tools the platforms use — pointed back at you.
Roll your own. Own the deck.

beats.narration_text: The SKILL.md anatomy. Frontmatter at the top names the skill and its trigger — whether Claude invokes it automatically or waits for an explicit slash command. The workflow block describes the steps. The Never section lists what the skill must not do. The Verification block says what a passing run looks like.
beats.new_visual_element: SKILL.md file anatomy — frontmatter / workflow / Never / Verification

beats.narration_text: The Never section is advisory — Claude tries to follow it, but it is probabilistic, not deterministic. If a rule must hold absolutely — for example, never generate a final grade — that enforcement belongs in a hook script, not a Never block. The skill defines intent. The hook enforces it.
beats.new_visual_element: Never section in SKILL.md — advisory vs deterministic callout

beats.narration_text: Babbage's insight: the unit of work is the named reusable procedure, not the per-session command. The skill is that unit. Build it once, invoke it every semester. The investment is forty-five minutes. The return is every invocation after.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — SKILL.md template + invocation economics

beats.narration_text: Build your first skill. Paste this prompt — Claude will scaffold the SKILL.md from your workflow description.

props.command: Help me write a SKILL.md file for a workflow I run every two weeks. I will describe the steps; you draft the frontmatter, workflow block, Never section, and Verification block. Ask me the questions you need to fill each section.

beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.
